ddls:
CREATE TABLE "colleges" (
  "college_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each college */,
  "college_code" TEXT /* Code assigned to the college */,
  "college_name" TEXT /* Name of the college */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the college */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Primary contact number for the college */,
  "email" TEXT /* Official email address of the college */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the college */,
  "established_year" INTEGER /* Year the college was established */,
  "accreditation" TEXT /* Accreditation status of the college */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description or overview of the college */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("college_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "courses" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course */,
  "course_name" TEXT /* Name of the course */,
  "stream" TEXT /* Stream to which the course belongs (e.g., Artificial Intelligence, Aeronautical Engineering) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the course in years */,
  "degree_type" TEXT /* Type of degree awarded */,
  "fees" TEXT /* Annual fees for the course */,
  "prerequisites" TEXT /* Prerequisites for the course */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the course */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "college_courses" (
  "college_id" INTEGER /* ID of the college offering the course */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course */,
  "seat_category" TEXT /* Seat category for the course (e.g., GM, KKR) */,
  "intake_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of students that can be admitted */,
  "admission_requirements" TEXT /* Specific admission requirements for the course at the college */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person for the course */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("college_id", "course_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "admission_statistics" (
  "admission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each admission statistic */,
  "college_id" INTEGER /* ID of the college */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course */,
  "admission_year" INTEGER /* Year for which the admission statistic applies */,
  "admission_count" INTEGER /* Number of admissions for the course in the given year */,
  "application_count" INTEGER /* Number of applications received for the course in the given year */,
  "rejection_count" INTEGER /* Number of rejections for the course in the given year */,
  "acceptance_rate" TEXT /* Percentage of accepted applications */,
  "average_gpa" TEXT /* Average GPA of admitted students */,
  "average_test_score" TEXT /* Average test score of admitted students (e.g., SAT, ACT) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("admission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_admission_statistics_college_id FOREIGN KEY ("college_id") REFERENCES colleges ("college_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_admission_statistics_course_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_id") REFERENCES courses ("course_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, staff) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON string or list of permissions associated with the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
